reasoning: October is typically the driest and hottest month in Cayenne, French Guiana (closest to the Equator), with daily average temperatures around 27.5°C. January, conversely, lies in the wet season, with heavy precipitation, extensive cloud cover, and lower daily averages around 26.1°C. Given a difference in monthly means of ~1.4°C and an average daily standard deviation around 0.8°C, the probability that a day in January is hotter than a day in October is essentially the chance of anomalous rainy weather suppressing temperatures on the October date while sunny weather elevates them on the January date. Statistically, this represents roughly a 10-15% chance.
